sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Bobby, presents with a 2-3 week history of right shoulder pain, which started after a period of intense physical activity in his backyard, including lifting and manual labor.
He reports the pain is located on the outside of his right shoulder and is more tender when he raises his arm.
He denies any numbness in his arm or hands.
He has a history of similar shoulder pain in the past, which he attributes to a previous injury playing baseball.
He has been managing his symptoms with ibuprofen and ice, which provides some relief.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of occasional shoulder pain due to a previous injury playing baseball.
Review of systems: No other symptoms mentioned.
Current medications: Ibuprofen (as needed) for pain.
Objective:
Vital signs: Blood pressure 116/84, heart rate 62, respirations 16, temperature 97.8, oxygen saturation 99%.
Physical exam: The patient has limited active and passive range of motion of the right shoulder, with tenderness at the greater tuberosity of the humerus.
No tenderness at the sternoclavicular or acromioclavicular joints.
Good hand grip and intact sensation to touch on the right arm.
Test Results: No fracture on shoulder x-ray.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has right shoulder pain due to rotator cuff tendinopathy, which is likely caused by repetitive strain from recent physical activity.
Problem list: Right shoulder pain (active); rotator cuff tendinopathy (suspected); bilateral foot pain (active).
Plan:
I ordered an MRI to further evaluate the rotator cuff tendinopathy.
I referred the patient to physical therapy for 6-8 weeks to address the tendinopathy.
I advised the patient to continue taking ibuprofen as needed for pain relief.
If symptoms do not improve, we may consider a steroid injection.
I recommended that the patient try soaking his feet in Epsom salts to alleviate foot pain.
Follow-up: The patient will follow up with me in 6-8 weeks to re-evaluate his symptoms and progress with physical therapy.
